title: Use REBOOT TO SETUP on the Hospital HMI When Updating HMI Date or Time
summary: This source-specific runbook captures the documented entry point and access restriction for using the REBOOT TO SETUP button on the Hospital HMI Default screen when an HMI date or time update is needed. The source only confirms where the control is located and that only maintenance personnel should use it; it does not provide the subsequent setup or date/time change procedure.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when access to REBOOT TO SETUP on the Hospital HMI is needed specifically for updating the HMI date or time, and maintenance-authorized personnel are available.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as an operator or general user.
Do not use this procedure for tasks other than updating the HMI date or time unless supported by another source.
Do not treat this runbook as the full date/time update procedure; the source excerpt does not provide the setup steps after using REBOOT TO SETUP.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Maintenance-authorized personnel identify and use the REBOOT TO SETUP button when an HMI date or time update is needed.

safety_notes: Only maintenance personnel should use the REBOOT TO SETUP button if they need to update the date or time on the HMI.
The source does not provide the subsequent setup or date/time update steps, so stop and escalate if those steps are required and not otherwise documented.

system_preconditions: Hospital HMI is available and the Default screen can be viewed.
The task is specifically to update the HMI date or time.

visual_references.description: Hospital HMI 2.4 Default screen showing installed software information, station IP address, login/logout access, and the REBOOT TO SETUP button context.
Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu showing access to the 2.4 Default screen.

steps.title: Open the Hospital HMI Default screen
steps.purpose: Reach the screen where the REBOOT TO SETUP control is documented.
steps.expected_result: The Hospital HMI Default screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The Default screen is visible and shows installed software information, the Hospital Station IP address, and login/logout access.
steps.failure_condition: The Default screen cannot be accessed or the displayed screen does not match the documented Hospital HMI 2.4 Default screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI 2.4 Default screen layout, including installed software information, station IP address, login/logout access, and the REBOOT TO SETUP button area.
Maintenance Menu access point for the 2.4 Default screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the Hospital HMI Default screen cannot be located.
Stop and escalate if the visible screen does not match the documented Default screen context.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Default" screen displays information related to the software currently installed and the IP address of the Hospital Station. Users can also log in to and log out from the hospital HMI.

steps.title: Locate the REBOOT TO SETUP button
steps.purpose: Identify the restricted control referenced by the source for date/time update access.
steps.instruction: Locate the REBOOT TO SETUP button on the Default screen.
steps.expected_result: The REBOOT TO SETUP button is identified on the Hospital HMI Default screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The REBOOT TO SETUP control is visible on the documented Default screen.
steps.failure_condition: The REBOOT TO SETUP button cannot be identified on the Default screen.

actions.label: Find REBOOT TO SETUP
actions.exact_action: Locate the REBOOT TO SETUP button on the Default screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the REBOOT TO SETUP button is not present on the Default screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Only maintenance personnel should use the REBOOT TO SETUP button if they need to update the date or time on the HMI.

steps.failure_condition: The requested activity is unrelated to HMI date or time update or cannot be confirmed.

steps.title: Restrict use to maintenance-authorized personnel
steps.purpose: Apply the source-stated access restriction before using the control.
steps.instruction: Have only maintenance-authorized personnel use the REBOOT TO SETUP button.
steps.expected_result: Use of the REBOOT TO SETUP button is limited to maintenance-authorized personnel.
steps.healthy_condition: A maintenance-authorized person is the one performing the action.
steps.failure_condition: An operator or general user attempts to use the REBOOT TO SETUP button, or maintenance-authorized personnel are not available.

actions.label: Use restricted control
actions.exact_action: Have only maintenance-authorized personnel use the REBOOT TO SETUP button.

success_criteria: The Hospital HMI Default screen is identified.
The REBOOT TO SETUP button is identified on the Default screen.
The task is confirmed to be an HMI date or time update.
Only maintenance-authorized personnel are identified to use the control.
The limited scope of the source is documented and follow-on setup steps are escalated if needed.
healthy_conditions: The Default screen displays installed software information and the Hospital Station IP address.
Users can log in to and log out from the hospital HMI on the documented Default screen.
REBOOT TO SETUP is used only by maintenance personnel for HMI date or time update access.
failure_conditions: The Default screen cannot be accessed or identified.
The REBOOT TO SETUP button cannot be located.
The requested task is not limited to updating the HMI date or time.
A non-maintenance user attempts to use the REBOOT TO SETUP button.
Personnel require the actual date/time update steps, but those steps are not present in this source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if date or time updates are needed but maintenance-authorized personnel are not available.
Escalate if an operator or general user is asked to perform this procedure.
Escalate for the actual date or time update steps because the source excerpt does not provide them.
